sessions.intent: v1 plan: OBP cleanup of PLang/App/ — sequence the refactor as thirteen independent shippable stages.

before.assumptions: Channels work (runtime2-channels v1) is shipped and may or may not be merged to trunk. Walked PLang/App/this.cs (681 lines), Channels/this.cs (277 lines), Modules/this.cs (464 lines) on the latest tip. Identified persistent smells: App.Run god method (85 lines, 8 foreign mutations), KeepAlive private collection enforced from outside, Modules.Describe on the wrong owner, dual Serializers storage (Stage 6 not finished), v1 channel-write residue, app.Debug.CallStack placement, GetStatic shim, Errors.App injection. New OBP rules sharpened in conversation: compound class names red flag (Rule A), Get<Plural>() missing-collection (Rule B).
before.risk: Plan churn if branch trunk question not settled. Stage ordering is opinionated — Ingi may reorder. This branch is the planning home only; implementation work will happen on per-stage cleanup-<slug> branches forked off the trunk.

actions.context: Spine: narrative why-thin-stages, thirteen-stage index in four tiers (close-drift / real refactors / bigger redesigns / hygiene sweeps), branch strategy (one branch per stage, runtime2-cleanup is planning home only), cross-cutting decisions, four open questions for Ingi.

OBP discipline reference: eight smell tests reproduced from CLAUDE.md with cleanup-specific examples; the two new architect-sharpened rules (compound-name red flag, Get<Plural>() missing-collection) with quick-screen grep recipes; per-stage anatomy (smell, realignment, new shape, files, risk, tests, out-of-scope); definition of done per-stage and for the whole plan.
Branch summary: scope, structure, two new rules, stage index table, four open questions, next step. Newest entry at top per architect convention.
Branch session report; this entry.

after.status: v1 plan ready for Ingi review on the runtime2-cleanup branch. Spine + principles topic doc + summary + report. No stage files carved yet — those come per-stage when approached.
after.notes: Four open questions block stage 1 carving. No code changes; pure planning session. Branch was forked from runtime2-channels tip on 2026-05-07; first attempt accidentally wrote into the channels branch's architect tree and was reverted before this branch was created.

sessions.intent: Add Rule C (static fields are a missing @this) to the OBP sharpened-rules and add a Tier 4 stage for the eviction sweep.

before.assumptions: Rule A (compound names) and Rule B (Get<Plural>()) already in plan/principles.md. PLang/ tree has ~5 static mutable fields and 81 static readonly — most of the latter are caches, JsonSerializerOptions, lock objects. Static methods (factory methods, conversion operators) are not in scope.
before.risk: Initial wording was too broad ('any static variable'); narrowed in conversation to fields-not-methods, with three carve-outs. Carve-outs need to stay tight or the rule becomes toothless. Walked PlangTypeIndex.cs to confirm the lock argument: lazy init creates the racing-thread problem the lock guards; once data has @this owner with deterministic construction, both the lock and the static-ness disappear.

actions.context: Renamed section 'The two architect-sharpened rules' to 'The architect-sharpened rules' (prevents future churn when more land). Added Rule C — static fields are a missing @this. Three carve-outs: const, AsyncLocal<T>, lock objects whose guarded data is irreducibly static (empty set on this codebase). Quick-screen grep included. Updated definition-of-done to add the static-field screen alongside the two-capital and Get<Plural>() screens.

Updated 'Stages 1–13' → 'Stages 1–15'. Added Tier 4 stage 15: static-state-eviction-sweep. Stage description flags the two hits that need design notes before the sweep runs (OpenAiProvider._requestCount on an actor-resolved provider — wrong scope; Test.run.ChildAppCreated static event — wrong shape) and the mechanical targets (PlangTypeIndex → App.Types.@this, JsonSerializerOptions singletons, ReservedKeywords).

after.status: Rule C captured and stage 15 carved as a one-liner. No code changes. Stage 1 still next; 15 is at the back of the queue per Tier 4 'last (against the settled tree)' positioning.
after.notes: Decision deferred: whether OpenAiProvider counter and Test.ChildAppCreated event should split out into their own design-discussion stages (Tier 3) or stay folded into stage 15. Will revisit when stage 15 is approached and the rest of the tree has settled.

sessions.intent: Draw the post-cleanup destination tree for PLang/App/ — give Ingi a visual artifact to judge the end state instead of reading 15 stages of prose.

before.assumptions: All 15 stages now defined. Walked PLang/App/ filesystem (full tree, 30+ folders), confirmed loose roots (Info.cs, View.cs), checked ambiguous files (Debug/this.cs is 748 lines and its scope survives stage 7 because only the property is promoted; Catalog has ExampleRenderer/ExampleHelpers as Rule A candidates not currently in stage 14's named list). Tree is best presented as a single annotated end-state, not two side-by-side trees, because the diff is what carries meaning.
before.risk: Several judgment calls in the tree are architect's lean, not settled fact: JsonSerializerOptions destination (disperse vs single home vs Channels/Serializers), ReservedKeywords destination (Variables vs own folder), OpenAiProvider counter (delete vs instance vs App-level), Test.ChildAppCreated shape. All flagged as open in the doc. PropertyFilters/ collection and Goals/Goal/Methods.cs partial-class rename are deferred — flagged for Ingi to decide whether to add stages.

actions.context: Topic doc: marker key, full annotated end-state tree, six open judgment calls with architect lean, summary of net adds/removes (4 new folders, 5 deleted files, 2 renames, 5 materially shrunk, 14 unchanged).
Linked post-cleanup-tree.md from the spine intro and from the layout block at the bottom.

after.status: Destination tree written and linked from plan.md. Six open judgment calls surfaced for Ingi: JSON-options home, ReservedKeywords home, OpenAiProvider counter handling, Test.ChildAppCreated event shape, loose roots Info.cs/View.cs, PropertyFilters/ collection.
after.notes: No code changes. Doc is the architect's lean, not a contract — every stage we land may push back on the tree. Architect-side: ready to start carving stage 1 (serializers-stage-6-finish) once Ingi has reviewed the tree.

sessions.intent: Address Ingi's six review comments on post-cleanup-tree.md (v1) and pull latest runtime2 (channels merge + io-channels docs).

before.assumptions: Branch was on the pre-channels-merge runtime2 base. Ingi posted a big multi-bullet review comment plus five focused ones on the tree. The merge from runtime2 brings in the channels work which already realised parts of stage 14: EventContext.cs renamed to Channel/Events/this.cs, MigrationEnvelope.cs deleted, Variables/Calls/ added. Some review bullets were rendered moot by the merge (Migration removal); some were sharpened (case-mismatch concern on JSON options, with a Rule C frame).
before.risk: The big bullet comment spans agree / disagree / clarify across 17 sub-points — single-status resolution wouldn't reflect reality. Posted comprehensive reply and left it open so Ingi can re-engage per bullet if anything in the rewrite missed. Provider→Code rename is a real design question parked as Open judgment call E. Catalog placement under Build/ is parked as B (waiting on Ingi to recall the prior thread).

actions.context: Full rewrite reflecting v1 review. Tree updated for: MemoryStepCache → Cache/Memory.cs; Callback/Signature/ folded into Callback/this.cs; Choices → Build/Choices/; PropertyFilters/{Sensitive,Transport,View}.cs collection; Json.cs/Text.cs renames; Data/Converter.cs; Snapshot/ISnapshot.cs; Test/{File,Run,Status}.cs; ReservedKeywords → Variables/Reserved.cs (all const/readonly); OpenAi counter → DELETE; Events/ collapsed (Lifecycle layer dropped). New 'Inline answers to v1 review questions' section walks each bullet. Open judgment calls revised: A (JSON home — shifted to single-home given case-mismatch concern), B (Catalog under Build/ — waiting on prior thread), C (Plang vs PlangData — depends on legacy mimetype usage), D (RestoredFrame → Call/Position low priority), E (Provider→Code design question), F (Events collapse blast radius), G (ChildAppCreated event shape), H (Info/View loose roots deferred unchanged).

Logged the OpenAiProvider._requestCount static counter as a temporary blocker per Ingi 2026-05-07. Stage 15 deletes the field, the MaxRequestsPerProcess const, and the increment-and-throw block. No replacement scope.

Posted six architect replies via the review server API. Four user comments resolved (JSON options, ReservedKeywords, OpenAi counter, PropertyFilters). Two left open intentionally — the big bullet comment (multi-status, can't be resolved as one) and ChildAppCreated (design TBD until stage 15 carves).

Pulled origin/runtime2 twice (channels merge + io-channels docs update). Worked around CLAUDE.md skip-worktree by saving harness overlay, lifting flag, checking out HEAD, merging, restoring overlay. Two merge commits land cleanly: 8d5a5b82 (channels merge) and 92a1e... (io-channels docs).

after.status: Tree v2 reflects all settled review feedback. Three substantive design questions parked open for Ingi: Provider→Code (concept change vs find-replace), Catalog placement (under Build/?), and Plang vs PlangData (legacy mimetype usage). Branch is up-to-date with runtime2; ready for stage 1 carve once design questions are settled.
after.notes: No code changes — pure planning. The Provider→Code question is the most consequential: my position is that it's only a good rename if it carries a story-level concept change ('runtime no longer talks about providers'), not a 88-file find-replace. Waiting on Ingi's read.

sessions.intent: Audit the prior-attempt branch (origin/runtime2-obp-restructure architect v1-v3) for missed insights, then absorb the ones Ingi accepts into principles + tree + plan.

before.assumptions: Ingi's response to the audit was point-by-point: agree on Rules D and E, agree Catalog dissolves into Modules.Schema (he confirmed runtime uses builder via the builder module), agree on MIME table split, defer audit-methodology and additional gap categories ('lets talk about this after'), source-gen update is moot due to recent rewrite, agree on Modules-out-of-scope rationale, light-touch on /shared/app-tree audit.
before.risk: Verified two facts before editing: (1) source generator no longer references App.Utils.* — confirmed by grep on PLang.Generators (Ingi was right, the LazyParamsGenerator.cs:638 reference is gone after the recent rewrite); (2) /shared/app-tree/ directory does NOT exist anywhere in the repo — never has — so v3's audit methodology is blocked on missing substrate.

actions.context: Added Rule D (gerund-named app-graph properties — Builder/Tester pattern with three-form table folder/property/CLI) and Rule E (decomposed parameters that should navigate — worked example Catalog.Build(modules) → app.Modules.Schema.Build()). Both with quick-screen greps. Updated definition-of-done to include both new screens.

Major v3-absorption pass: Catalog dissolves into App/Modules/Schema/ with Spec/Action.cs + Spec/Example.cs (per Ingi's L235 comment). Build → Builder, Test → Tester (Rule D). Channels/Serializers/Formats/ added (extension table). Types gains Clr(mimeType) overload; Registry.cs and Conversion.cs partial siblings. Choices marked tentative (★) for re-evaluation post-Catalog. Open question B resolved (Catalog dissolves into Schema). New open question I (audit substrate missing). Net stats updated: 9 folders added, 2 folders renamed, 14 files renamed, more files deleted via Catalog dissolve.

Stage 9 renamed: 'modules-to-catalog-lift' → 'catalog-dissolve-to-modules-schema' (different shape: Catalog disappears, content goes under Modules/Schema/). Stage 16 added: builder-tester-rename. Stage 17 added: mime-table-split. Stages 1-15 → 1-17. Updated cross-cutting decision #3 to reference Rules A-E (was just 'two new rules').

Replied to Ingi's L235 ExampleSpec/ActionSpec comment (id 7cadb12b26) confirming the Spec/ subfolder shape, marked resolved. Two user comments still open: the big bullet comment (intentional) and ChildAppCreated (design TBD).

after.status: v3 absorption complete. Five sharpened rules now active (A compound-name, B Get<Plural>, C static-fields, D gerund-property, E decomposed-parameters). Stage list grew to 17. Catalog dissolves into Modules.Schema; Build/Test → Builder/Tester; MIME table splits cleanly. Three open design questions remaining: Provider→Code rename (E), Plang vs PlangData mimetype (C in tree's open list), audit substrate (I).
after.notes: Discovered /shared/app-tree/ doesn't exist — v3's audit methodology is blocked. Flagged in tree as open question I with three resolution paths. Architect lean: tightening rules covers a lot of what the audit would catch; (a) building the surface files is too much overhead unless we commit to a long-term doc artifact. Waiting on Ingi's call. Choices placement also flagged tentative (★) — re-evaluate after Catalog dissolves to see if the build/runtime border has shifted enough that Choices wants a different home.

sessions.intent: Split rule material per Ingi's distinction: audit recipes go in Documentation/v0.2/audit/; per-bot OBP lens suggestions go in mounted drive /shared/bots/obp/. Existing OBP docs unchanged.

before.assumptions: Ingi's distinction between audit material (find smells in existing code) and authoring material (don't write smells in the first place) is structurally correct. The 5 sharpened rules with grep recipes are audit material; the foundational principles + per-bot workflow guidance is authoring material. Existing /CLAUDE.md and Documentation/v0.2/good_to_know.md already cover the foundational 4 smells and the OBP naming principle well — additive work, not restructure.
before.risk: Per-bot lenses could drift from the shared core over time. Mitigated by keeping lenses small (~50 lines each, workflow framing only) and pointing at core.md for principles. Bot lens docs are suggestions in mounted drive, not committed proposals — Ingi's call on whether to formalize them through the proposal mechanism later.

actions.context: Audit folder. README explains when to use (end-of-refactor sweep, onboarding, specific-smell investigation) and how to extend (new pattern → add the screen + filter + worked example). obp-rules.md has all 5 sharpened rules with full grep screens, filter recipes, today's signal/noise counts on PLang/App/, and worked examples linked to current cleanup stages.

Per-bot OBP lens suggestions in mounted drive. core.md is the consolidated source of truth (foundational 4 + sharpened 5 + naming principle + how-the-rules-relate). Each lens (~50-80 lines) is workflow-specific framing that points at core.md for principles. Coder lens: 'when you write a class.' Architect lens: 'when you design.' Codeanalyzer lens: 'what to flag, severity guidance, what NOT to flag.' Tester lens: 'OBP shape and testability, where to expect tests, smells that affect test design.'

after.status: Audit folder committed to Documentation/v0.2/audit/. Per-bot lens suggestions in /shared/bots/obp/ (5 files, mounted drive — not committed). Existing OBP docs unchanged. Branch ready for stage 1 carve when design questions settle.
after.notes: Open question for Ingi: when to read through the lens suggestions and decide whether to formalize via proposal mechanism. My lean: end-of-refactor, when we have real cleanup-experience to validate the lens content against.

sessions.intent: Final review pass before context clear: address Ingi's new comments on the tree (Position.cs, Plang/ folder, App/Json/ pushback, ExampleHelpers, comment-tool issue) and update summary.md so re-onboarding is clean.

before.assumptions: Ingi wants to clear context. Plan needs to be in shape that a fresh architect can re-load cleanly. Five new comments on the tree to settle first. Most critical pushback: App/Json/ as root mount was wrong (Json is a format, not a domain) — must revert that v3 decision.
before.risk: Low. All artifacts are durable. Only thing genuinely open after this session: Provider→Code rename (still needs Ingi's read), ChildAppCreated event shape (stage 15 design).

actions.context: Tree updates: (1) App/Json/ removed — JsonSerializerOptions disperse to consumers (Ingi: format not domain). (2) Channels/Serializers/Serializer/Plang/ subfolder added with this.cs + Data.cs (room for Protobuf.cs later). (3) RestoredFrame → Call/Position.cs settled. (4) ExampleHelpers.cs deleted entirely (record constructor covers the use case). Open questions A, B, C, D all resolved; F still open (low priority); E (Provider→Code) still needs Ingi.

Added new entry at top covering 2026-05-08 work: 5 rules (was 2), 17 stages (was 14), audit folder + per-bot lens artifacts, effectiveness audit results (rules catch ~5 of 17 stages mechanically), open questions resolved/remaining, re-onboarding path for post-clear architect.

Posted 4 architect replies. Resolved 3 user comments (Position.cs + Plang folder; App/Json drop; ExampleHelpers delete). Left 1 open intentionally — the comment-tool meta-issue (line drift) is a real tool problem and the original ChildAppCreated comment underneath it stays open until stage 15.

after.status: Plan is ready for context clear. Durable artifacts cover everything: plan.md spine + 17 stages, principles.md with Rules A-E + foundational 4, post-cleanup-tree.md destination, audit folder, per-bot lens suggestions in /shared/, summary.md log with re-onboarding path. Stage 1 (serializers-stage-6-finish) ready to carve.
after.notes: Two genuine opens remaining: Provider→Code rename (Ingi's call), ChildAppCreated event shape (stage 15 design TBD). Neither blocks stage 1. Comment-tool line-drift issue surfaced but isn't architect work.

sessions.intent: Carve stage-1 brief (serializers-stage-6-finish) for coder handoff.

before.assumptions: Plan, principles, tree, summary all settled and renumbered post coder-handoff cleanup pass. Stage 1 = drop the Serializers carry-overs on Channels.@this and Channel.Stream.@this; route through App.Serializers (canonical singleton already exists at App.this.cs:154).
before.risk: Stream channel needs App reference at WriteCore/ReadCore time — Channel base class already exposes App via internal setter, so the wiring works. v1 helpers in Channels.@this (also touch Serializers) get cleaned in stage 2; stage 1 routes them through App.Serializers in the meantime rather than deleting them.

actions.context: Stage brief: drop Channels.@this.Serializers + Channel.Stream.@this.Serializers carry-overs; route 4 internal call sites through _app.Serializers / App.Serializers. External callers (Goals, DefaultFileProvider, Setup) already use app.Serializers — no migration. Smell #3 (same logical thing stored twice across types).
Plan.md stage 1 row links to the carved brief and notes carve date.

after.status: stage-1 brief carved and committed; ready for coder handoff
after.health: plan + principles + tree + summary all consistent post-renumbering and audit; no unresolved planning questions
after.notes: Stage 1 brief covers two file edits (Channels/this.cs, Channel/Stream/this.cs), one ctor-parameter removal, four internal call-site updates. Out-of-scope items explicitly named (v1 helpers → stage 2; ReadAsync<T> → stage 8; renames → stage 15). Coder reads plan.md → principles.md → stage-1 brief and starts.
